participants.reasoning: The extracted participant description matches the official criteria on age, smoking restrictions, primary moderate-to-severe dysmenorrhoea, and baseline pain score, with only minor differences in reporting the analyzed sample versus randomized/completed counts.
participants.extracted: Women aged 18–40 years (≤30 years for smokers with a daily cigarette consumption of ≤10) with moderate-to-severe primary dysmenorrhoea were enrolled. Moderate dysmenorrhoea required OTC analgesics during menstruation with significant pain relief and symptoms interfering with usual activities; severe dysmenorrhoea required analgesics with inconsistent relief from OTC analgesics, prescription analgesics in at least some cycles, and discomfort causing inability to work or carry out usual activities. Eligibility also required a self-rated sum pain total score of at least 8 on the Andersch and Milsom dysmenorrhoea grading scale over the two baseline menstrual cycles. In total, 223 treated women were included in the full analysis set (115 flexible extended regimen, 108 conventional regimen); baseline characteristics were similar between groups, with mean age about 25 years.
participants.official:
Inclusion criteria: otherwise healthy females with moderate‐to‐severe primary dysmenorrhoea; prospective self‐rated sum pain score ≥ 8 during the 2 baseline cycles; aged 18–40 years (inclusive) with smoking habits as follows: aged 18–30 years of age, daily cigarette consumption ≤ 10; > 30 years of age, no smoking

interventions.reasoning: The extracted text describes the same EE 20 μg/drospirenone 3 mg extended flexible regimen and conventional 24/4 regimen over 140 days, with only minor wording differences and added rescue ibuprofen.
interventions.extracted: Combined oral contraceptive pill (ethinylestradiol 20 µg/drospirenone 3 mg) given in two regimens: a flexible extended regimen with a minimum of 24 consecutive active tablets and then continued daily up to 120 consecutive days, with a 4-day tablet-free interval initiated after 3 consecutive days of bleeding/spotting during the flexible phase; and a conventional regimen of five 28-day cycles over 140 days, each cycle consisting of 24 active tablets followed by a 4-day hormone-free interval (hormone-free tablets). Ibuprofen was permitted as rescue medication.
interventions.official:
1. Extended flexible regimen of EE 20 μg/DRSP 3 mg; triggered by bleeding events (oral tablet)
2. EE 20 μg/DRSP 3 mg administered in conventional regimen (24 days active + 4 days placebo)
For 140 days

outcomes.reasoning: The extracted outcomes cover the same core efficacy and safety domains as the official list, with only minor wording/detail differences and no substantive mismatch.
outcomes.extracted: The trial planned to measure the following outcomes: Primary efficacy outcome: number of days with dysmenorrhoeic pain over 140 days, with pain recorded daily in a diary as spasmodic pelvic/lower abdominal pain associated with withdrawal and/or menstrual bleeding episodes and severity rated on a 4-point verbal scale (0=no pain, 1=mild with no need for analgesic, 2=moderate with need for analgesic, 3=severe with need for analgesic), assessed over the 140-day treatment period. Secondary efficacy outcomes: days with at least moderate dysmenorrhoeic pain (daily diary/verbal rating scale; 140 days); days with dysmenorrhoeic pain associated with withdrawal bleeding (daily diary; 140 days); days with dysmenorrhoeic pain associated with unscheduled bleeding (daily diary; 140 days); days with pelvic pain independent of vaginal bleeding (daily diary; 140 days); days with dysmenorrhoeic pain that interfered with daily activities (daily diary; 140 days); bleeding profile, including number of days and episodes of bleeding/spotting by 90-day reference periods, bleeding intensity (none, spotting, light, normal, heavy), and proportion of subjects with withdrawal and intracyclic bleeding during individual cycles (90-day periods and individual cycles); investigator-assessed global improvement in dysmenorrhoea using a 7-point Clinical Global Impression-type scale from 1=very much improved to 7=very much worse (end of study/over treatment period); and user satisfaction using a 7-point scale from 1=very much satisfied to 7=very much dissatisfied (end of study/over treatment period). Safety outcomes: adverse events, metabolic parameters, carbohydrate metabolism, vital signs, clinical laboratory tests, and physical and gynaecological examination findings, assessed during the treatment period and at study end.
outcomes.official:
Primary: number of days with dysmenorrhoeic pain
Secondary: use of rescue medication; interference with daily activity; number of days with at least moderate dysmenorrhoeic pain; number of days with pelvic pain; number of days with dysmenorrhoeic pain associated with withdrawal bleeding; number of days with dysmenorrhoeic pain associated with unscheduled bleeding; bleeding patterns; assessment of treatment
